reasoning: The Lean statement correctly encodes "V is a K-vector space" via `Field K`, `AddCommGroup V`, `Module K V`. The spanning-by-countably-infinite-set hypothesis is captured precisely by `Set.Countable S ∧ S.Infinite ∧ span K S = ⊤` (Mathlib's `Set.Countable` means at-most-countable, so combined with `S.Infinite` this gives countably infinite). The conclusion `Countable ι` (from Mathlib's `Data.Countable.Defs`) means "at most countable", exactly matching "finite or countably infinite". The encoding of "every linearly independent subset" via universally-quantified families `R : ι → V` with `LinearIndependent K R` is equivalent: any linearly independent set T gives rise to the family indexed by T (subtype), and conversely, since `Field K` implies `Nontrivial K`, linear independence of R forces R injective, so ι is in bijection with its linearly-independent image in V. No hypotheses are silently added or dropped.
